1.1: No existing data will be reused. All data will be collected specifically for this research.

During the project, four main types of data will be generated: raw data from microarray experiments, data from ontological analysis, working data generated during laboratory work (e.g., digital lab notebooks), and secondary data resulting from the interpretation and processing of raw data.

The project will generate: survey responses from approximately 200 participants collected through online questionnaires (tabular data, approximately 50 MB); interview recordings: 20 semi-structured interviews (60-90 minutes each), approximately 10 GB of audio files; transcriptions and coded data from qualitative analysis.

The research will produce microscopic images of biological samples: approximately 500 images captured using a confocal microscope at 100x and 400x magnification, stored as TIFF files, total size approximately 2 GB.
Measurement data from spectroscopic analysis: time-series data from approximately 100 samples, totaling approximately 5 GB of data in CSV format.
1.2: The formats of saved files can be divided into two types: formats specific to laboratory software, such as .ndf (NimbleScan), .ixo (Light Cycler 96), and commonly used text (.txt, .docx), tabular (.csv, .xlsx), and graphical (.tiff, .png) formats.

Data will be stored in the following formats: .csv for tabular data, .txt for transcriptions and documentation, .py for Python analytical scripts, .pdf for final reports, .tiff for microscopy images, .png for charts.

Working data will be maintained in Microsoft Excel format (.xlsx) during active analysis. Upon project completion, all datasets will be converted to the open CSV format (.csv) to ensure long-term accessibility.
To ensure the broadest possible access, data intended for repository deposit, both raw and processed, will be saved in open file formats – provided this does not result in information loss.
2.1: Raw data, due to the specifics of laboratory software, may have different sets of metadata that will not be modified in any way. The main tool for ensuring consistency will be laboratory notebooks, maintained in both paper and digital versions.

All data deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) will be described with attributes compliant with the DataCite standard. Metadata descriptions will be stored in JSON-LD format and will include, where possible, persistent identifiers (PID), such as ORCID.

Each dataset will include a README.txt file containing: description of research methodology, explanation of all variables with units and value ranges, data collection procedures, file and folder structure, software requirements, known limitations, and contact information.
2.2: Raw data from individual experiments will be thoroughly documented in laboratory notebooks. Equipment calibration will be performed according to manufacturer protocols. Data entry will be verified by two independent persons. Validation scripts will automatically check value ranges.
Quality control includes: double data entry, monthly equipment calibration, automatic value range verification scripts, maintaining laboratory notebooks in both paper and digital versions.
3.1: Data will be stored on institutional servers with automatic backup (daily), on an external hard drive (weekly, stored in a different location), and in the institutional OneDrive cloud (automatically, in real-time). 3-2-1 strategy: 3 copies, 2 media types, 1 off-site location.

Active data will be stored on a laboratory drive and synchronized with an institutional server. Backups will be created automatically daily to the server and manually weekly to an encrypted external hard drive.
3.2: After project completion, data will be deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) at Gdansk University of Technology, which holds CoreTrustSeal certification. Minimum retention period: 10 years after project completion.
Data will be deposited in a certified repository ensuring long-term storage in accordance with FAIR principles. Data will be made available with full documentation (README, DataCite metadata).
4.1: The research does not require ethics committee approval because it does not involve human participants or personal data. All data will be collected from publicly available sources or generated computationally.

The research was approved by the institutional ethics committee (approval number: [NUMBER], date: [DATE]). Participants will be informed about the research purpose and will provide written consent. Personal data will be processed in accordance with GDPR based on participant consent. Data will be anonymized before sharing.
The data does not contain personal or sensitive information. All data collected from participants will be anonymized during collection, making identification impossible.
4.2: The project does not involve any legal issues regarding intellectual property or confidentiality agreements. All data is owned by the institution and will be shared under an open license.

Experimental data is owned by the institution in accordance with Intellectual Property Regulations. There are no legal restrictions on sharing. Partner data may require a short embargo period – this will be agreed upon with partners.
5.1: Data will be made publicly available under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0) license, requiring attribution. Sharing will occur no later than at the time of research results publication.
Data will be deposited and made available in the MOST Wiedzy repository under the CC BY 4.0 license immediately after project completion, no later than 6 months from the grant end date.
Data requires a short embargo period (maximum until [DATE]) due to publication preparation. After this date, it will be fully publicly available under the CC BY 4.0 license.
5.2: Data will be deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) at Gdansk University of Technology – the only certified research data repository in Poland (CoreTrustSeal certificate), ensuring FAIR-compliant access.
Data will be deposited in the thematic repository [NAME] and/or in the institutional MOST Wiedzy repository. The choice is dictated by compliance with funder requirements and FAIR principles.
5.3: Data will be made available in open formats (.csv, .txt, .png) with full documentation (README, DataCite metadata), enabling reuse by other researchers.
Source code will be published in a GitHub/GitLab repository under the MIT license, enabling reproduction of analyses and results.
5.4: Data is shared as open data under the CC BY 4.0 license and can be reused by anyone, provided attribution is given.
Due to the nature of the data (confidential data, sensitive data), access will be possible after submitting a request and obtaining consent from the data owner.
6.1: Data management costs include only working time: estimated [X] working days for documentation preparation, quality control, and deposition. The MOST Wiedzy repository offers free storage for Gdansk University of Technology employees.
Data management costs: working time estimated at [X] working days; institutional repository storage – free of charge; purchase of external backup drives: approximately [AMOUNT] PLN.
6.2: Data management is the responsibility of the principal investigator [FULL NAME] (position: [POSITION]). Substantive support is provided by the Open Science Competence Center for DMP verification and metadata.
Data management is a shared responsibility of all research team members, coordinated by [FULL NAME]. Data management procedures are described in the project plan.
